The '''Assault Frigate Mark I''' was a modification of the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class cruiser]] that was used in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:IsThisANebulon.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Assault frigates attack the [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']].]]
As the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] began to acquire [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]-era [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]] ''Dreadnaught''-class [[Starship/Legends|starships]], it found the required 16,000-being crew to be difficult to manage and procure, yet they were in desperate need of [[Warship/Legends|warships]].<ref name="SOTG" />

After equipping ''Dreadnaught''s with [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and automated systems to reduce the crew requirement to 5,000 beings, thus rendering the ships usable, Rebel engineers began modifying ''Dreadnaught''s to allow for greater speed and efficiency while retaining the original weapon systems. The final product was only recognizable as a descendant of the ''Dreadnaught''-class by the nose of the ship. Most of the internal structure was exposed to space, while a dorsal and ventral fin provided added maneuverability. The modifications resulted in greater fuel efficiency.<ref name="SOTG" />

These modifications removed the internal docking capacity of the ''Dreadnaught''-class, but the ships were equipped with 20 umbilical docking fixtures like those on the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]]. Unlike the Nebulon escort frigate, however, these docking fixtures could not carry other ships through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="RAS2" /> In addition, the [[Assault frigate/Legends|assault frigate]] carried a modified [[Gamma-class assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]] on its back, which was secured in a manner that allowed it to be carried in hyperspace.

The assault frigate had more weapon emplacements than the original Dreadnaught, but the [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] had a shorter range and slower fire rate due to the increased demand on the main power system. The removal of large quantities of hull plating reduced the ship's durability, but slightly stronger [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] partially compensated for this.

The Assault Frigates generally had one prong at the bow, but variants were equipped with split bows.<ref name="SOTG" />

==History==
Around [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="TFU2 project" /> a twin-pronged variant Assault Frigate fought in the Rebel Alliance's [[assault on Kamino]]. The ship reverted from hyperspace along with the rest of the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] ships to face three [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]].<ref name="TFU2" />

Few of the ships were retrofitted by the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], but their effectiveness meant that the class had no reported losses.<ref name="SOTG" />

Assault frigates first saw combat around the time of the [[assault on Kashyyyk]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. They served as a mainstay of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|Defense Fleet]] in the years following the Battle of Endor.

[[File:Rebel_Assault_Frigate.png|thumb|left|300px|An Assault Frigate Mark I fought in the assault on Kamino.]]
The frigate was tweaked into the [[Assault Frigate Mark II/Legends|Assault Frigate Mark II]], and although the Mk. I remained in operation, the Mk. II production line failed.

One Mark I frigate was used to make up a [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|secret retreat]] in [[137 ABY]].<ref name="SWL25">[[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:AssaultFrigate-SOTGse.png|thumb|right|125px|An image from [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'']] in [[2007]]]]
The Assault Frigate Mark I included in [[Starship Battles (Star Wars Miniatures)|Starship Battles]], published in [[2006]], presents a different image of the Assault frigate, featuring a slimmer shape overall, as well as two forward hull sections that look identical to that of a [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] and are arranged in the same manner as the [[Nebulon-B2 frigate|Nebulon-B2]]. [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy Saga Edition'']], published in [[2007]], also presents this style of Assault Frigate.
